body_txt: A Cliffhanger without the Cliff. Sometimes, as a television producer or writer, you want the viewer to wonder "What Happens Next?" when a season is over. One way to do this is a Cliffhanger ; but Cliff Hangers are a Cyclic Trope , or maybe your series, which is already cliffhanger-heavy , wouldn't stand up to another cliffhanger, or maybe you're worried that you won't last another season. That's where the "What Now?" Ending comes in. Instead of leaving the protagonists in mortal danger, this ending leaves them broken, or gone, or what have you. The "What Now?" Ending allows the three months to a year to pass between new episodes in "series" time as well, if the writers so desire. Also, a "What Now?" Ending allows a form of insurance against Real Life Writes the Plot ; actors who die or are unavailable can be handled by having something happen to the character during the Time Skip . If the Five-Man Band is broken up, or the hero leaves the City of Adventure , or the wife of the protagonist leaves him, that's a "What Now?" Ending. Not to be confused with So What Do We Do Now? , And Then What? , or Now What? , a different kind of "what now ending". Also an ultimate form of "Shaggy Dog" Story . Since this is an Ending Trope , expect spoilers.
